old_claim.note: Casting and molding was treated as an enabling capability for precious-metal working without direct edge-level source support.

new_claim_absent: No direct casting-and-molding dependency remains because the node is scoped to native gold and silver working, including ornaments and value storage, not to cast-metal production as the required mechanism.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract and introduction: the review places gold and silver evidence within early Balkan metallurgy and dates early gold and silver, without making casting and molding a direct prerequisite.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports early precious-metal working in Balkan and Aegean contexts, including gold and silver chronology.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Casting and molding is a neighboring metalworking process; keeping it direct would overstate the mechanism for native gold and silver working.

ontology_before: Gold and Silver Working depended directly on Casting and Molding, implying cast-metal production was required for the scoped precious-metal node.
ontology_after: Gold and Silver Working stands as a source-backed precious-metal-working node without a direct casting-and-molding prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: casting_and_molding is no longer a direct prerequisite for gold_and_silver_working.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to cast precious-metal object production rather than native precious-metal working.
A stronger source showed casting and molding directly enabled the earliest scoped gold and silver evidence.
A new dependent needed gold_and_silver_working to imply casting technology rather than precious-metal material use.
short_reason: Casting is adjacent process context, not the source-backed direct prerequisite.
